source: Paraphrased coverage ledger for The Book of Elon's 69 Core Musk Methods chapter

methods.concept: Assume your capability can exceed your current estimate.
Ordinary people can choose extraordinary standards.
Teach yourself through broad reading and expert contact.
Begin fallibly and update toward being less wrong.
Internalize responsibility for the outcome.
Real supply depends on making real things.
Useful products and services are the basis of wealth creation.
Prefer a useful life to status alone.
Seek meaningful work rather than glory.
Choose actions that improve the odds of a good future.
Innovation rate decays unless renewed continuously.
Look for work that has only recently become possible.
Build an obviously needed thing before consensus arrives.
Pursue valuable things others are not building.
Visible proof can attract allies as the work advances.
A prototype converts belief into proof.
Start, question assumptions, and adapt to reality.
Reason from fundamentals instead of imitation.
Estimate the theoretical ideal before optimizing reality.
Compare component cost with finished-system cost.
Question, delete, simplify, accelerate, then automate.
methods.anchor: question requirements, delete, simplify, accelerate, automate

methods.concept: For critical work, use a daily algorithm review cadence.
Stay close to the work and consequences of decisions.
Treat requirements as challengeable recommendations.
Separate physical constraints from inherited conventions.
The best unnecessary part or process is absent.
Simplicity can improve both reliability and cost.
Find the design necessity behind every survivor.
Overdelete experimentally, then add back only proven needs.
Seek radical breakthroughs where incremental change cannot close the gap.
Take proactive ownership of strategy.
Use urgency as an operating principle after direction is sound.
Doubling factory speed can approximate doubling capacity.
The active bottleneck controls overall throughput.
The weakest critical supplier can set system speed.
Run independent work in parallel.
Give a team one decisive scoreboard metric.
Integrate design, engineering, and manufacturing.
Compete on the speed of useful innovation.
Win through speed, quality, and cost.
When a goal seems absurd, ask what it would take.
Exceptional engineers are often the scarce resource.
Spread chief-engineer systems understanding across the team.
Create a direct feedback loop with reality.
Prefer demonstrated ability over ego.
Stop effort that does not improve the product or service.
Train product taste through deliberate observation.
Emotional discomfort does not change physical reality.
Empathy cannot substitute for job-relevant evidence.
Communicate with simple, clear, humble language.
Investigate through the closest available source.
Hire using evidence of exceptional job-relevant ability.
Combine engineering judgment with financial fluency.
Product leadership may require company-level authority.
Lead visibly from the frontline when the situation requires it.
Move attention and presence to the current problem.
Escalate bad news quickly and repeatedly until understood.
Permit learning failures while protecting catastrophic boundaries.
Distinguish fear of failure from evidence-based stop conditions.
Feel fear, inspect it, and act when evidence supports action.
Double down only after renewed evidence and bounded downside.
Reserve extreme effort for work important enough to justify it.
Serious commitment requires caring about the mission and accepting bounded hardship.
Important work does not excuse preventable harm.
Low odds alone need not rule out an important bounded bet.
Persist strongly while retaining explicit stop conditions.
Use a game frame to make difficult progress more playable.
Treat ultra-hardcore intensity as optional, bounded, and rare.
Use humor to make communication and products more distinctive.
